en_body: Desert. Fields of burned grass...Wild oases in loneliness...Villages lying hushed, clusters of houses curling themselves round, closing themselves within one another's souls. Whenever a door opens slightly, the hut built of grasses and straw mingled with the binding substance of clay releases again the emblems of one survival: human bodies containing the fierce fullness of human Life! Oh! Africa, Africa, O Africa: what civilization has dragged Nature into the dark abyss of discrimination and the degeneration of Humanity?
note: The closing question indicts colonial civilization for turning racial discrimination against both African humanity and the natural world.
